sum(f-) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)

prose:
Fukui reactivity indices for formaldehyde were computed using DFT at the B3LYP/def2-TZVP level of theory (tier `standard`, density-fit) with PySCF. The calculation was performed in the gas phase with a charge of 0 and a multiplicity of 1. The geometry was sourced from `/home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/formaldehyde/formaldehyde.xyz`.

- The most nucleophilic site (highest f- value, indicating susceptibility to electrophilic attack) is the oxygen atom (atom 1), with an f- value of 0.46742.
- The most electrophilic site (highest f+ value, indicating susceptibility to nucleophilic attack) is the carbon atom (atom 2), with an f+ value of 0.70839.
- The most radical site (highest f0 value, indicating susceptibility to radical attack) is the oxygen atom (atom 1), with an f0 value of 0.46778.
